task_completion.description: Select the 'Body' object in the FreeCAD Tree View.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Body' item is highlighted (usually with a different background color) in the Tree View panel on the left side of the FreeCAD interface.
An 'Export file' dialog window is open and visible on the screen.
The file path or breadcrumbs in the Export dialog indicate the current folder is 'FreeCAD' inside 'Documents' inside 'ga'.
The text 'exported_model' or 'exported_model.stl' is visible in the 'File name' or 'Name' input field of the Export dialog.

task_completion.description: Select the STL file format for export.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Save as type' or format dropdown in the Export dialog is set to 'STL Mesh (*.stl *.ast)' or a similar STL option.

integrity.description: The agent used the FreeCAD GUI to perform the export.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with FreeCAD menus (File -> Export) and dialogs, rather than terminal commands or Python console scripts.

integrity.description: The correct model is open in FreeCAD.
integrity.visual_evidence: The T8 lead screw housing bracket (a 3D part with a pad, holes, and polar pattern) is visible in the FreeCAD 3D viewport.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply create a dummy file using a file manager or text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The export action is clearly happening from within the FreeCAD application window.
